phase3_fixes.error_text:
ERROR MESSAGE:
"No AMF is associated to the gNB" (logged by NGAP module)

fix_suggestion.reason: The error 'No AMF is associated to the gNB' is caused by the gNB failing to find or associate with a valid AMF instance. This is often due to misconfiguration of the AMF IP address or the gNB IP address used for NGAP communication. The NGAP module logs this error when no AMF is found in the gNB's AMF list or when the NG setup procedure fails. The call graph shows that the NGAP layer attempts to select an AMF but fails, leading to warnings and errors. The functions ngap_gNB_nnsf_select_amf_by_amf_setid and ngap_gNB_nnsf_select_amf_by_guami return NULL when no matching AMF is found, which triggers the error. The configuration parameters GNB_IPV4_ADDRESS_FOR_NG_AMF and amf_ip_address in cu_gnb.conf are currently set to IP addresses different from the deployment context, causing the gNB to fail to reach the AMF. Correcting these IP addresses to match the deployment context will allow proper AMF association and NG setup.
fix_suggestion.config_fix: Update cu_gnb.conf to set amf_ip_address to ({ ipv4 = "192.168.70.132"; }) and GNB_IPV4_ADDRESS_FOR_NG_AMF to "10.138.77.131" to match the deployment context. This ensures the gNB communicates with the correct AMF IP and uses the correct local IP for NGAP.

config_patches.description: From commit 431edb6189c4fc177f5bb9b942a22b1c35a2105f: Fix gNB configuration issue
From commit 431edb6189c4fc177f5bb9b942a22b1c35a2105f: Fix gNB configuration issue

fix_suggestion.root_cause_analysis: The root cause of the error is that the gNB is configured with incorrect IP addresses for the AMF and for its own NGAP interface. The gNB attempts to select an AMF for UE context setup but fails because no AMF with the configured IP and parameters is found or reachable. The NGAP layer logs 'No AMF is associated to the gNB' when the AMF selection functions return NULL. This prevents successful NG setup and UE context establishment. The mismatch between configured IP addresses and the actual deployment context IPs causes the NGAP association to fail, leading to the error.
investigation_steps: Verify the IP address configured for amf_ip_address in cu_gnb.conf matches the actual AMF IP (192.168.70.132).
Verify the IP address configured for GNB_IPV4_ADDRESS_FOR_NG_AMF in cu_gnb.conf matches the gNB IP (10.138.77.131).
Check network connectivity between gNB and AMF IP addresses (ping, traceroute).
Check NGAP logs for NG setup request and response messages to confirm association attempts.
Confirm that the AMF is running and listening on the configured IP and port.
After correcting IP addresses, restart gNB and monitor NGAP logs for successful AMF association.
fix_suggestion.specification_context:
3GPP SPECIFICATION REFERENCE
================================================================================

**Section: 5.3.3 RRC connection establishment (Page 60, Section 5.3.2.3 & 5.3.3.1)**
UE initiates RRC connection establishment via RRCSetupRequest, receives RRCSetup, and responds with RRCSetupComplete.
On failure to establish RRC connection, UE increments numberOfConnFail (max 8) and informs upper layers.
If upper layers abort RRC connection establishment before RRC_CONNECTED, UE stops timer T300, resets MAC, releases MAC config, and re-establishes RLC for all RBs.
Relevant for ensuring proper RRC connection setup with AMF association via NGAP signaling.
**Section: AMF-Identifier Information Element (Page 347, Section 6.3.1)**
AMF-Identifier (AMFI) includes AMF Region ID, AMF Set ID, and AMF Pointer as per TS 23.003 clause 2.10.1.
Correct AMF-Identifier configuration is critical for gNB to associate with the correct AMF.
Misconfiguration or absence of AMF-Identifier in NGAP messages can cause "No AMF is associated to the gNB" error.
**Section: General RRC Procedures and Message Processing (Page 53, Section 5.1.2)**
UE must process received RRC messages in order and complete processing before next message.
Proper handling of setup and release choices in RRC messages is mandatory.
Correct setting of rrc-TransactionIdentifier in responses matching network requests is required.
Ensures synchronization and correct state transitions during RRC connection setup and AMF association.
**Section: Paging and RRC Connection Resumption (Page 60, Section 5.3.2.3)**
UE reacts to Paging messages by matching UE identity and initiating RRC connection resumption with appropriate resumeCause.
Proper resumption procedures ensure UE can reconnect to network and AMF after idle/inactive states.
Failure in resumption or paging handling may indirectly cause AMF association issues.
**Summary for Fixing "No AMF is associated to the gNB":**
Verify correct AMF-Identifier configuration in gNB NGAP setup messages.
Ensure RRC

commit.author: chinchans <vangalachandu77@gmail.com>
commit.body:
Fix gNB configuration issue

Error Message: "No AMF is associated to the gNB"

Applied 1 code patches and 2 config patches.

Selected Patch Details:
- Code: select_amf (ngap_gNB_nas_procedures.c) (ngap_gNB_nas_procedures.c)
Description: Adding logging to help trace the AMF selection process and identify if the GUAMI is being processed correctly.
- Config: GNB_IPV4_ADDRESS_FOR_NG_AMF (cu_gnb.conf) (cu_gnb.conf)
Description: Must match deployment context value to ensure the gNB can reach the AMF.
- Config: amf_ip_address (cu_gnb.conf) (cu_gnb.conf)
Description: Must match deployment context value to ensure the gNB can correctly communicate with the AMF.
